review_highlights.comment: This teacher taught me Calculus 3 via MIT OpenCourseware because my actual teacher can't teach.
Excellent Professor for Multivariable Calculus

The first time Math 18.02 L01 Multivariable Calculus is very difficult, but this professor explains good. It's class covers a lot Topological Questions, Maxwell Equations . Be aware Is a very tough grader. The exams are hard and is difficult to get an A .

Math 18.02 Multivariable Calculus (Calculus II ) at Massachusetts Institute of Technology is excellent with this Professor. Sadly he is at UC Berkeley.  Explains extremely well, and Master the Material I love the physics applications in the Maxwell Equations. He covers Partial Differential Equations, the Laplacian , Fast Fourier transform.

I took Math 18.02 Multivariable Calculus ( Calculus II) with this Professor. He explain everything clear and is very accesible outside of the Class . Is very Sad that he is in UC Berkeley. You learn so much with him. The Exams are though but you always interested in Mathematics with his Class .

analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: If I could go back to the past, I would gladly not go to MIT, which was my dream school at the time, just to not have him as a professor.

Professor Whittle is the best I have ever met. His courses are just enjoyable. The logic and organization are clear, and the story engaging. This course provides generous amount of reading that directly pushes you to confront the original data where all the theories are based. Your understanding about soil will be brought to a whole new level.
Extremely intelligent with great knowledge of the material. Tough grader though..

review_highlights.comment: Asu is really nice as a person and is super friendly and cares about her students and her work a lottttttt.. but imo her work is not that good is just okkk. Many times her papers are just simple trivial extensions. Be careful and find your right balance

I enjoyed my interactions with Asu. From what I knew, she really cares about her students, and works very hard to be hands-on in research (than many senior profs I knew at MIT). I found her research work illuminating and inspirational, with some of them definitely playing leading roles in her domains. Great professor to work with as a student imo.
She is soooo insanely over rated as a researcher....   Idk how she is at MIT...  Her advisor really hurt the field so much by appointment her as a prof here....

analysis.name: Alan Adams
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Online and free. Im 55 yrs old and wish he was my teacher back in the day.

Profesor Alan Adams has a lot of enthusiasm teaching physics. The exams are a little hard and he don't accept assignments late .  He is an Expert in Quantum Mechanics at Graduate Level. I love so much its course .
Fantastic Prof Exams are pretty difficult but after you see the amount of effort he puts in, you'll like to put the required effort to do good in these difficult exams.

I watched his lectures on OCW, and every time they were funny, fast-paced, and occasionally groundbreaking. His lecture on indistinguishable particles left me speechless, and I need time to pause the video and recover. The way he built off basic logic and arithmetic to prove the Pauli Exclusion Principle was mind blowing. Thank you Professor Adams
I am sad I only got to see one of his courses.

The course is a little bit Hard but is very interesting have a lot of Mathematics and beautiful graphs. If you have The chance to take and Economics course with him . Take it
Is a Excelent professors in Economics . I like Econ 14.03 he explain extremely well The Microeconomic Theory and Public Policy. Study The course have a lot of Mathematics but is very interesting

Economics I thought that will be very boring, but was a truly nice experience. I took Intermediate Applied Microeconomics  14.03 and was a wonderful experience. But I major in Nuclear Science and Engineering and Physics. Economics is Quantitative Social Science. Massachusetts Institute of Technology is well known in Economics

An Amazing Professor who is always there for you. Even though the math and the graphs are hard, he made every effort to make it understandable with his amazing team of TAs. Whenever you have a question, Prof. Autor quickly responds to clear our confusion even on weekends.
Made the topics harder to understand than they needed to be. Had to read and do other courses to actually learn the material. Watch out for his frequent misstatements when he does math.

review_highlights.comment: By clicking the "Submit" button, I acknowledge that I have read and agreed to the Rate My Professors Site Guidelines, Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. Submitted data becomes the property of Rate My Professors.

this class is a nightmare. Assignments are unnecessarily convoluted and feel like busywork. Grading is harsh and inconsistent, with no real feedback. Avoid this professor if you value your GPA and sanity.

Professor Azizi was also very responsive to questions and concerns, both in the discussion forums and during office hours. They provided timely and helpful feedback, which was invaluable in improving my understanding of the material.
i can't even eat tacos in his class, GOD HELP ME

review_highlights.comment: Massachusetts Institute of Technology Department of Economics is One of the Departments with More Nobel Lauterates. Econ 14.32 Econometrics with Professor Mikusheva is a very interesting Class . The Exams are difficult and She loves so much Statistics. I took before this Class Math 18.440 Probability and Help me but i was Lost in Econ Applied .

Econ 14.32 Econometrics with Professor Ana Mikusheva is extremely difficult. She explains well but you have to read the book. The exams are most difficult than the book exercises. This course is a lot of Probability and Statistics. She test heavy . Econometrics is very hard but interesting.

Tough and demanding class, but teaches Econometrics in such a way that you will find it useful no matter what you do afterwards - that being economics, computer science or reviewing poor clickbait research articles. The lectures was good paced and relevant for the exam, and the grading is fair.

review_highlights.comment: The class is a flipped classroom style, so the professor doesn't really matter. He occasionally appeared while our group was working and gave vague feedback that left us more confused. Outside of the classroom, he was horribly uncooperative. He changed course policies frequently and never had a helpful response when students asked questions.

Boring lecture videos. Gives ambiguous questions. Course could be more structured and more formal. Comes to our table, does not look at our response, starts 'lecture' on why we must come to class prepared.
great Professor  he allows his students to share  and receive much needed information. Loved him

analysis.name: Barton Zwiebach
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Barton has raised the pedagogy bar to levels I never knew existed in academia.  He is by far the best professor I have ever had.

The perfect physics professor. A rather unique blend of knowledge with occasional jokes making the session worth its while. His lectures are lucid and should be suffice if you really pay attention. Heck he makes Quantum Mechanics look easy.
You can revamp 8.05 ten more times, but I WILL STILL LOVE YOU BARTY! Super clear and organized explanations that will make you believe physics is easy (although the tests may change your mind :) )
The ideal teacher. All lectures were concise and exceptionally clear. The homework provoked reflection and a deeper understanding of course material. One of the best physics classes I have taken.
best physics teacher ever, best accent ever.

The best professor for Principles of Chemical Sciences 5.111 at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

I took general chemistry and organic chemistry with this professor. I would take any class this professor offered. Dr. Taylor could make a class on slicing bread the most organized and profound experience of your professional career.

Chem 5.111 is Principles of Chemical Sciences at Massachusetts Institute of Technology. The class is very interesting. She really cares about your learning chemistry. Is a difficult course but is beautiful connected with other sciences like biology, quantum mechanics, and the chemistry of the universe. Excellent Professor

Dr. Taylor is an incredible teacher. As an instructor, she gets to focus all of her time on teaching, and it shows in her lectures. They were all very clear and well-organized, and therefore very easy to understand. She made me really enjoy organic chemistry.

This guy rocks!!  Very laid back....

A math teacher that will walk through the steps of problem solving, but not demonstrate significance of the mathematics at hand. Lots of equations with not much meaning. He is a really funny guy who can engage the audience.
ONLY from his name u can understand his stupidity! bravo!!! CHANGE UR NAME MAN! PLEASE!     ghs babis cosmmin

All she cares about is writing difficult exams. I didn't get the vibe that learning was important.

This class felt thrown together as if it was just an afterthought. The exams are a mangled mess of questions from other classes with many of the problems having absolutely nothing to do with what was taught.

I like its class

John Belcher is one of the Greatest  Professor of Physics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.  You come to MIT to be challenge . So many students cry because he failed me . He don't failed you. You failed yourself. If you're not ready for MIT go to University of Puerto Rico Mayagüez.

I thought he was great.  Helpful, nice, really cares about teaching and helping you to learn.  If people complain it's because they didn't want to take physics in the first place.  Given that it's a requirement, you should do your best to get him.
Prof. John Belcher is one of the Professors from Physics that Challenge you . He is a genius physicist but you need to spend to many hours in their class . Sofía
His lectures could be better but he's really helpful if you go to him for help outside of class. He'll spend a lot of his time figuring out what you don't understand and clarifying.

If you have the chance, take this course quick (and if you want to get a good grade, bring some menthol oil, to clear his airways)
this guy's kinda bad... i didn't like his teaching. would not recommend.

I took Chem 5.111 Principles of Chemical Science that have Calculus and the Chemistry concepts by Prof. Cummings are not presented very clear. He is very smart but the level of difficulty if you don't study well can be hard. If you're not Major in Chemistry take this in next semester
refused to release practice materials for the final and insisting on exams of unfinishable length.

Somewhat convoluted lectures, hard to follow his train of thought. Probably too smart to teach an intro level class. WAY too much focus on WHO did what and when vs. the actual chemistry. Has a very monotonous voice. You will want a good TA to explain the more abstract concepts.
Very unclear about the subject in itself. Knowing about Nobel Prizes don't help you learn what you ought to know.

she's a really cool, fun professor..animated, interesting, plus her tests werent too bad
i found her to be really hard to talk to and unapproachable.
If I ever have insomnia one night, I should invite Prof. Drennan over to give me a lecture. I'll be asleep in one minute. Drennan talks in a monotone, and did a horrible job of keeping my attention.

A nice guy. Listening to him is very interesting but sometimes he skips too many major historical events in favor of trivial ones. He also goes on tangents a lot.
very clear, animated teaching. encouraged class discussions. he really sparked my interest in american history, and legal history in general.

review_highlights.comment: His explanations aren't as clear as I expect them to be

I have been taking his MIT online courses in China for quite a bit of time already. He is the best teacher I have ever had. He has great lectures and makes everything easy to understand. He gives great examples of problems to make sure everyone understands. If you're looking for a strong basis in math, go take his courses!

I took the online course on single variable calculus and was so impressed with his teaching. All my life I've hated math but he made me fall in love with calculus, he made it appear so easy and intuitive. He is an exceptional professor.

Math 18.03 Differential Equations is a very beautiful class . The course use a lot of examples from Engineering, Physics, and other Sciences.  The Professor is great , he teach you to see beyond Differential Equations. The class is the most hard math requirement for an Engineering student, but is very interesting.
He's amazing professor. i've learned single variable calculus from him, via mit ocw, and it helped me   a lot

Esther Duflo is the best professor of Economics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology. She is very caring about its students . She do amazing lectures and help the students to grasps concepts.  She use the Technology and the Challenge of World Poverty is a wonderful topic . Inequality economics is a problem.
Amazing class with incredibly amazing profs.
took the online openmitware course, the best time of my life

I took 8.02 Electricity and Magnetism despiste The course is Hard he made so Easy to understand. He colaborates de  with The students and The Class is truly inspirational. Helps a lot and have passion for teaching Physics .

Professor Peter Dourmashking is one of The Best Professors at Physics Department at Massachussetts Institute of Technology. I took 8.02 Electricity and Magnetism despiste The course is Hard he made so Easy to understand. Always collaborates with The students and The Class is truly inspirational. Helps a lot and have passion for teaching Physics .

Physy 8.01 Classical Mechanics is one of The most amazing courses at Massachussetts Institute of Technology. The review lessons and extremely helpful . Physics is The most difficult course at The freshman year. He is Truly inspirational and pretty clear to understand. Help Students a lot. I took Physy 8.01 and 8.02 and was a amazing Experience .

Professor Dourmashkin exceeds expectations in every category as a teacher and a human being. He displayed a knowledge of physics unparalleled to almost any teacher I have ever had with any subject. He put in so much time and effort into helping us learn, which for a college professor is uncommon. Not only that but he cares about the students.
One of the best teachers I have ever had. Smart, funny, personable, and knows how to connect material at different levels. Physics was made into a fun and interesting class.

analysis.name: Daniel Sanchez
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Sanchez is an awesome professor! Very few people in CSAIL have the level of understanding of their area that he has and can communicate it so effectively to students! The man is awesome. There is no topic on comparch you can ask him about that he doesn't know about. Highly recommended!

004 is a super cool course! Prof. Sanchez is really really knowledgeable and cares a lot about the students. The lectures are 50 min so sometimes he has to go fast, but I went to a couple of his OH and he was super helpful in explaining things I didn't understand. His other course, 6.823 is also awesome and u learn more about modern architectures!

Loved this new version of the class! I had heard horrible stories about the old version but they've changed it and the new labs are fun to do and the language is not hard to understand. Prof. Sanchez is fantastic!

What a wonderful class! Professor Sanchez spends a lot of time with students to make sure each student is caught up to speed with the material. Great with newer students and very intriguing during lecture.
Good class, has been renamed to 6.191. Professor Sanchez and the other professors are all very good. Clear grading criteria.

analysis.name: Yonina Eldar
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: True that she is a full-time prof in Israel. But legit to review her here because she is a visiting scholar in MIT and inflicts misery on everyone who comes in her contact and everywhere she goes. She loses temper over small things—would scream, yell, curse in group meetings. Her language and behavior aren't constructive at all.

Did my thesis with her. Lab environment was toxic and dysfunctional, perpetually on the brink of implosion due to her anger management issues. Interactions with her were characterized by relentless shouting, particularly during group meetings and phone calls. When frustrated, she would SCREAM, that could be heard a few blocks down the hallway.

I found interactions with Yonina surprisingly empty and utterly devoid of genuine connection. She constantly evaluates you based on how useful you are to her agenda. Once you've fulfilled your purpose or if you express inability to meet her demands, her interest in you plummets immediately. Her behavior is very transactional and manipulative.

Yonina thinks she can BS her way to fool all students and lure them to join her lab or get them to write a paper on which she can put her name. You will not learn anything from her. She only has superficial knowledge and tries to skip details or delegate them to someone else - and its true, do not expect her for any help in your career.

Yonina was complained about so much in Technion that the institute decided to go with the age-old "pass the harasser" solution. They just let her to be hired by Weizmann so that she can stop terrorizing the engineers, staff, colleagues, and students in Technion. I'm sure many in the department were relieved to see her leave.

analysis.name: Erik Demaine
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: ERIK DEMAINE IS FANTASTIC!  I want to be him when I grow up.  Fascinating research and a really engaging lecture.  I would take any of his classes, even if they're not in my department (18).

A pretty decent professor, however, there are some issues when attempting to learn certain concepts. He uses 3 very standard examples for dynamic programming that do not build good intuition. I spent most of my time in the class just watching Tim Roughgarden's youtube videos. A genius professor, just slightly stuck-up.
I am a student who always ends up listening to his lectures when I start studying for my class quiz or when I have to do my assignment. Amazing lectures. Always awed by the way he drives the class.
was good all through the class

He Explain genetics, Microbiology, Biochemistry and other Topics like different Ecosystems all in one Class . Massachussetts Institute of Technology is The best University and Its shown by The Quality of Its Professors
Excellent Professor of Biology so fun.  I studied NSE biology is very useful and easy

Bio 7.012 is Fundamentals of Biology at Massachussetts Institute of Technology. Its lectures are amazing. If you read and Study you Pass with an A. He is The Best Professor of Biology and explain Genetics like no One can. Definitely Take you learn so much and he is very intelectual. Biology was The Best course at The First year.

Clear, exciting, inspiring, thought provoking and riveting lectures. Difficult concepts for many. The software employed is helpful for understanding the concepts. Eric introduces concepts as things to be worked out. You think along with scientific development. Genomics - emphasized.

Here's the deal - I hate biology. I've tried to enjoy it, it's just my least favorite subject. HOWEVER, Eric Lander somehow made dragging myself out of bed to go to a lecture on my least favorite subject worth it. God bless you, Professor Lander

He is always willing to stick around after class for discussion and has the most fascinating stories. He is one of the most knowledgable and well-read people I have ever met.

He is really boring.  He focuses on the most mundane details.  The articles he assigns could put an insomniac to sleep. He makes me never want to take political science again, and I was extremely interested in it before. Don't take it.
class was semi-boring.  he has high respect for students and does a good job teaching, interesting topics, pretty good class, graded fairly
Not the best. Class was pretty boring. Give 17.40 a miss.
LOVED his classes, interesting, and so clear.

Enectali Figueroa Feliciano was a great teacher In physics. He looks like Marc Anthony teaching Physics. He is from Puerto Rico . He studied at Stanford University. He is an expert in Dark Matter and Astrophysics in General. He challenges you . But is not the greatest at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.
He is a nice professor but he is also a TOUGH one.

review_highlights.comment: Franz has harsh requirements for grading but he's such a fantastic lecturer that it works out. Single-handedly the reason I liked this class as much as I did.
Please do not take a mechanical engineering class if it is taught by Franz. It will be much harder than when it is taught by a different instructor (he said so himself). 89.5 is a B, 79.5 is a C.
Hover is very knowledgeable of the subject material. TAs are not as good at tying p-sets to material.

analysis.name: Sara Fisher-Ellison
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: During the lecture she only reads slides, without explaining much. The test wasn't too hard, but she is very scrict in grading.

She fumbles and mumbles in nearly all of her sentences while delivering a lecture, making it extremely difficult to focus and follow along. The concept notes are rather jargon-heavy, and examples hardly convey the underlying concept clearly. 

It is nearly painful to follow along in her class, luckily it was 14.310x and online.

Probably a genius in her field, but somewhat pedagogically challenged. She reads off her slides, uses incredibly technical language, does not read the room, and still makes very many mistakes. In addition, examples are unnecessarily complex and confuse rather than illustrate concepts.

Frequent misstatements, mistakes and missing content in notes, instructions to recall from several lectures back without explanation, and inadequate detail in explanatory math. The few examples focus on testing calculus & notation expertise rather than building intuition. Little integration of concepts. Books full of errors and ambiguity.

I think it is very inspirational that a woman is teaching 14.01 at MIT.  It would be even more inspirational if two women in charge of the subject.  However, Ellison shouldn't teach any lectures during 2023 unless the scandal blows over.  Students will be thinking, what's going on with Caroline this week?  Need to invite a visiting professor again.

analysis.name: Gordon Freeman
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I took Dr. Freeman for advanced spatial void tessellation theory and quantum finite element analysis and I really enjoyed his lectures. It's refreshing to have a professor that is so experienced with field work. I'm considering taking his advanced  crowbar manufacturing techniques lab as well, I've heard nothing but good things about that class.
Great class, but he didn't show up for the third semester for some reason. Otherwise, a great teacher and by far my favorite part of the MIT experience.

By far the greatest mind of our lifetime. This Professor is a man of few words but an incredible understanding of physics and trans-solar crustaceans. Free crowbars are passed out to everyone as well. The only gripe with him that I have is his lack of understanding of time; he never quite finishes anything.
Freeman was a bit on the quiet side. Dosent talk much. Showed us his pictures from Xen and his HEV. 33

Gordon is a genius.  He shows you how to swing your crowbar such that it will kill a head crab in one shot!  He has a sexy beard / gotee.  The ladies can't get enough of him.  Of course the G-man very rudely interrupts the class to take him away on those "jobs" he's always doing.

review_highlights.comment: Peter FISHER is one of the best professor of physics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.  He is an excellent lecturer. Enjoyable and nice person . Teach you so much physics
He makes fun of us for respecting horrible professors.  And makes fun of millenials.  And most people in general.  And is the only physicist I have ever met to like his family, and that is cute.
He made recetations really fun as he is really funny... He was also quite helpful in office hours, and was easily available to contact.
My god, when I took this class, I could barely stay awake.  Lectures were absolutely horrible..  plus they had that german guy who knew three words of english..  Godd job MIT!!

analysis.name: Fred Harris
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Fred rocks... just. rocks.  He really cares about music and his students... he wants everyone to be the best they can be, but also respects life at MIT, and encourages music to be a good outlet for stress.  Great guy!  Great teacher!

Dr. Harris guest conducted a music festival, wherein I played jazz drums for his band. Knowing and respecting that Fred is a percussionist, he and I had creative differences, and his mistrust for my interpretations created some issues. He chopped my parts into overly simple, cheesy playing, and approached the free language of jazz restrictively.

analysis.name: Woodie Flowers
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Flowers exudes a wealth of experience and knowledge, but he is too condescending and full of himself to inspire in my opinion. Rather than provide constructive feedback his approach is one of criticism.
Flowers is a very experienced and talented lecturer. Take his class!

Woodie is supportive and helpful, and also inspires brilliance.  He shows how much he enjoys product design and passes this enthusiasm to his students.  One of the best classes in MIT, and a must for all course 2 students- this class rocked my world.

analysis.name: Gilbert Strang
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 72 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Learned more in one 45 minute lecture from this guy than I have in about 20 hours from my actual professor and his notes. Mr. Strang has a knack for appreciating that to learn math and understand it we must first conceptually understand it before blindly repeating techniques. Clearly he understands the material far better than most professors.
The best professor for Linear Algebra at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.  He gives a lot of applications
Very useful videos on the internet!!!

Is The Best Professor for Linear Algebra especially to Engineering, Computer Science and Economics Applications . He explained very well but The Exams are extremely difficult .  He loves Linear Algebra and he dont understimate The ability of the Student to learn Mathematics. I like The course so much i majored in Economics and was truly inspiration

I have watched his online lectures on linear algebra, computational sc. and engineering 1 & 2. Wow.  All his lectures are a kind of original. I think this originality is what makes his lectures wonderful. Each time he is teaching his mind is active as if he himself is finding things out for the first time. His lectures are never cliche.

analysis.name: John Gabrieli
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very friendly and personable. 9.00 is a great class and I recommend it highly. It's fun and interesting and the grading is not too difficult. Course 9 makes an effort to make their classes effective at teaching and learning.

I followed his introductory psychology lectures on YouTube because I was not entirely pleased with the professors at my own university for the same type of course and I felt overwhelmed. Gabrieli made it fun and easy to follow, lots of relevant examples - it is very clear he knows what he is talking about. Look them up on YouTube, worth it!

I'm not an M.I.T. student. I watched the lecture series to introduction to Psychology out of interest. Proff Gabrieli is very funny and enthusiastic. You can tell he loves what he does. Gives lots of examples, great guy, a must take.

analysis.name: Wendy Gilbert

review_highlights.comment: Instructor seemed frustrated and irritated while answering students' questions during lecture.  Avoid at all costs.
A poor instructor who was clearly not invested in the success of students.
No, I don't want to write a review.

review_highlights.comment: He's my dad and he is a great dad.
An awesome teacher. But i felt the coursework was a little heavy.

analysis.name: Manya Ghobadi
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The worst instructor at MIT! She mostly talks about unrelated topics and interrupts others when they are speaking, which seems to be due to her lack of knowledge. She spends time going over the history of the field which you can find with a Wikipedia search, while not discussing important technical content. She is definitely not suitable for MIT.

She taught this course in an abnormal way. The course had a lot of guest lectures by people from other universities and the industry. This approach caused the course content to be very disorganized and prevented the class from covering the main aspects of the course content. She was very poor at teaching and acted more like a coordinator.

In terms of teaching, she is the worst instructor. She is unable to explain technical subjects clearly. In this course, more than half of the lectures where by guest lecturers from other universities and companies. This disrupted the course content and confused the students. This course with her was very bad experience.

Her lectures are just for her showing off. She speaks a lot (endlessly) about pointless subjects with a squeaky voice which gets on your nerves. She is the wrong person for this position at MIT. You will not learn anything from her lectures/recitations, which are a disaster. Avoid her seriously!
Manya's class is amazing. She was caring, kind, and extremely helpful throughout the course. Her optics lecture was the best. She helped our team a lot with the course project.

analysis.name: Haynes R. Miller
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Haynes R Miller is inspirational and a Genius in Mathematics. I love Math 18.900 Intro to Topology The course is very difficult but not is by The Professor. If you read and do your Work dont have Problems to Pass . Is one of The Biggest Names at Massachussetts Institute of Technology. He explains pretty clear You learn a lot

In Spring 2007 I repeat the wonderful experience that I had in 18.014 . I took 18.024 Multivariable Calculus with Theory that is Calculus II proof emphasize. The course is extremely gorgeous but challenging. Professor Miller explains everything clear . Multivariable Calculus, Differential Equations Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations.

I took Math 18.03 Differential Equations with Professor Haynes Miller . The personality is not the best but he teach you Mathematics well. Is not so accessible and the exams are harder . I like the course because he integrated so many of Linear Algebra topics. The applications are in Engineering and Science mostly .

Prof. Miller taught me to do elegant proofs in 18.014 Calculus with Theory. He is a great professor you learn so much but have to put efforts . He do proofs very natural and is a brilliant Mathematician . Sofía

In 2006 I took Math 18.014 Calculus with Theory with Professor Haynes R Miller . Is an excellent Professor he explained extremely well but the class is extremely beautiful and difficult. We used Tom Apostol Book that has two Volume This Course cover a lot of material From Single Variable Calculus, Multivariable Calculus, Linear Algebra and ODES

analysis.name: Hartley Jr. Rogers
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I had a good laugh at this class when I went.  I think Hartley Rogers might be just a little past his prime.  Halloween was priceless, though when he stopped mid-lecture and just said "booooooooooooooooooooo."  Maybe you had to be there, but an 80 year old man whose voice goes that low is hilarious.

He was my recitation 18.034 (diff equations) instructor in 1967.  Rather than throwing a blizzard of theory at us, he taught us how to do the work.  Novel concept!  He passed in 2015 but deserves mention here I believe.

Hartley is wonderful.  Class is entertaining, so sit back and enjoy the show.  Many find his class helpful for catching up on sleep, but why would one wish to miss the humor.    Hartley for President '04!
"My advisor (Alonzo Church) told me I should do computers, but I didn't believe him, so I did math.  Boy was I wrong.  I should've done computers!"  -Hartley Rogers.  Old, but hilarious as hell.
The class was very easy. Hartley Rogers was very entertaining. It was the last time that he taught 18.022.

analysis.name: Hong Liu
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Super clear lecturer. I feel that he sometimes leaves out details to focus on the bigger picture, which is fine at times, but can also sometimes feel unsatisfying. Nonetheless, he is a very good teacher and was very helpful during office hours. He's also just easy to talk to and quite friendly.

One of the few physics classes where I attended every lecture. Liu is a mathematical physicist so his perspective on QFT is less applied then I wished, but his development of the subject was complete and beautiful. I learned something new every class and Liu's answers to questions always provided one with more insight into the subject.
Hong Liu is truly the best professor I've ever had. He's truly the GOAT. His son is cool, too
My favorite  professor  and class at MIT.

review_highlights.comment: His understanding of engineering appears somewhat limited, making it noticeable.
His lectures are a bit confusing, but cram a huge amount of signals/systems/instrumentation into your head in an impressively short amount of time.

Frankly, lectures were all over the place. Much of the class was sitting around in a circle discussing our opinions about the reading material. I use these terms lightly, because it wasn't a very scholarly discussion - lots of stories. I understand Japanese Popular Culture is in itself not super scholarly, but I was hoping for more structure.

not a bad lecturer...not a bad guy...engaging lectures....

Professor Sawin is the most ill-mannered person in the Department of Chemical Engineering, and possibly in the whole Institute. He is extremely unhelpful and it can take him months to return an email if he doesn't like you for whatever reason he may have.
very helpful and accessible outside of class

analysis.name: Ikue Shingu

review_highlights.comment: The worst professor I have ever had. Avoid at all costs. She's super strict and will tease students in class for having trouble. Course design is impractical. It ruined my love for Japan and its culture. The anxiety from her affected my health and I had to drop. It was a traumatizing experience. Btw, she's at Harvard Summer school.

Had not consulted with the department head after starting their first academic year at NC State. Students were tested on the understanding of culture which counted between 25 and 30 percent of their test grades - never explicitly stated. Worked hard to best in this class by getting a tutor half way

One of the greatest teachers ever! She's really energetic and funny every class, and really cares about all of her students. Great explanations and her lessons always hone in and make the grammatical patterns very clear.

Students in the class vary in fluency and comprehension. Students consistently have had trouble with the work load. The professor didn't decrease the work load. The grading of all written assignments were far more strict than all except one faculty member. through the semester.
Shingu Sensei is amazing.  Amazing teacher, and amazing looking.  ONe day we will run away together to Osaka, and get married by a Emperor Hirohito impersonater.

I didn't really need to do much outside studying other than reviewing the homework and lecture notes. He also has humor.

analysis.name: Jackson Lu
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Professor Lu is absolutely wonderful! His genuine concern for student learning shines through in remarkable ways—he actually prepared customized feedback for each of the 150 students in our course to help us grow academically. Having him as our professor is a real privilege.

The course material and delivery are extremely effective and engaging. Professor Lu goes beyond his teaching responsibilities and even helped me with my job offer negotiation outside of class -- I am so grateful!
Each of Prof. Lu's lectures is like a TED talk! I love that he uses hilarious videos to demonstrate important points covered in class.
This class is A M A Z I N G! the right balance between theory and frameworks and applied to cases. One of my best classes of the MBA for sure

The lectures were uninspiring and reiterating the content of the readings, when approached out of class he was unhelpful and dismissive, grading was arbitrary and feedback incredibly vague. Avoid his classes. They take up all your time while offering little educational value. Nightmare professor and quite frankly a callous and spiteful person.

WORST PROFESSOR EVER!!! Do not take a course with him if possible, don't even converse. He has a total inability to understand the useless concepts that he blabbers about. I had him as recitation instructor for 8.03 and 8.05. He's a great guy, but while I understood the lectures, recitations made me more confused. Must be the Nobel

She's smokin'.  And I guess she's good as a prof too.

best class i ever took at the tute....

analysis.name: Zhuoxuan Li

review_highlights.comment: zhuoxuan is an extremely talented CS teacher. He is organized, clear, and loves his material. His lectures inspire you to love computer theory too. But he and his TAs have an extremely punitive grading style that makes one dread this course. Learning should not be punishment. Computer Science should not be about gatekeeping.

analysis.name: Louie Wurch
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had Dr. Wurch for BIO150 lecture and he was amazing. The class and class material wasn't stressful or too much to handle. I think the only reason I got a C in this class is because I'm not a strong test taker. I recommend him to anyone who isn't naturally good at BIO.

If every college professor were like Dr. Wurch the world would be a better place.  Most professors do not actually do their job as professors they just spew their knowledge for students to recite at a later date.  Dr. Wurch actually does his job to help students actively learn. Always willing to help and clarify any questions. Clear on expectations
Soooo good!! Loved the class and the recitations were the HIGHLIGHT of my week
Louie is GREAT!! Literally the greatest TA of all time.

analysis.name: Steven Leeb
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Leeb is probably the greatest lecturer and all around teacher I've ever been lucky enough to be in a room with. I left every class excited and finished each lab feeling 10x more capable than before with a true knowledge of the material. He cares about his students in a way we all felt deeply. I'm truly grateful to have been taught by him.

Was only able to attend the first few class sessions and did not register and complete the course for personal reasons/issues, but this professor is well known for good lectures and interesting assignments.
Wasn't sure which lab to take - within 2 lectures I was sold. Absolutely the best class I've taken at MIT. I totally agree with the class description - This class is the reason you came to MIT.
I had a lot of fun in this class!  Leeb got me interested in digital electronics, which I previously did not care much for.  He's always enthusiastic and definitely knows how to teach.
I came with no experience building: barely soldered, never used test equipment or a breadboard or microcontroller, never read a datasheet, never used C. The best class I've taken.

Financial Markets one of The most imperativo al Courses from Finance (15-3) Curriculum with Andrew lo very Inspirational
He is a good professor that has a new perspective and veiw on teaching and is a delight to talk to I really loved his lectures

great professor, extremely knowledgable and interesting!

This guy knows his stuff. Explains difficult subject matter in an easily digestible format. And if all that fails, he's extremely helpful during office hours Read his book Our Mathematical Universe, it's great

Max is not just hot, but also an incredible Physicist and Human being!
he really knows his stuff, he cares a lot about his students and explains things very easily and profoundly.

Although this is one of the hardest courses you can take, Max makes the calsses pass by quickly, he's really funny, engaging and Theoretical, Would recommend!
Tegmark is great! He really cares.
Great guy. Would take course again.

analysis.name: Moe Win
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 66 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Really great professor! Very willing to sit down to talk over conceptual questions after class or any time, even outside of office hours. He maintains rigorously consistent notations, which really helps with learning the finesse in statistics. Finally, he maintains a set of study guide for each topic which are textbook grade. Highly recommend!
He is a great professor and explains the contents in a clear and insightful way. All his lectures are very informative and clear.

His lectures were very clear, thorough, and balanced among giving students a good sense of theory and fundamentals, real-life applications of topics, and examples of how to solve problems. And all the TAs are very patient and helpful. I would highly recommend take a class of this professor.

This course is very organized and helps me obtain a deeper understanding in how modern communication systems are designed.  In addition, Prof. Win is very nice and patient.  He is very willing to discuss about the important concepts and technical details with the students.

Prof. Win is a great teacher. He explains the contents in a clear and insightful way. Besides the knowledge, he also focused on the methodology and philosophy behind the solution. I've been benefited a lot in his class.

analysis.name: William Minicozzi
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: He is not the most fantastic lecturer ever, but he covered the content without too many hitches (considering it was his first time teaching the class, it was good. I'm expecting he'll get better.) He is also HILARIOUS. He has a great personality and sense of humor. If you go to office hours, he is also quite helpful there. Highly recommended!
He was a good and clear lecturer, sometimes a bit fast. Makes jokes sometimes, seems like a friendly guy. It was possible to miss lecture and still get by in the class but not ideal.
Minicozzi is the best. He's hilarious and his lectures are pretty clear. 18.02 was great with him.
lectures are good and exams are just like the review

His lectures are hilarious. Yes the material is all posted online, but I show up to classes to listen to his sassiness :) Tests aren't incredibly difficult; they are (like he says in lecture) very similar to the practice exams posted online.

very good professor, difficult but helpful when needed.  Difficult tests, but very good teacher and fair grader.

review_highlights.comment: Best thing about her lectures are that anyone can easily understand as they all start from scratch.
The presentations were interesting.  The pdf board notes and summary notes were the most useful.
Very Clear lectures with good lecture notes for the on-line class
Simona is awesome!  Great lecturer.

Excellent professor! Very clear and informative lectures. She seems to really enjoy the course (elements of structures) and discipline (mechanical engineering) which compels me to be more interested and committed!

The course is More Statistics than Probability . Toughest Exams and All is explain in Power Point. He dont use so much The blackboard. Is better buy The book and read The lectures notes.

review_highlights.comment: I took math 18.03 Differential Equations with Prof. Alar Toomre the class is so vague and the Professor don't explain so well. I learn more in other Math Courses . I decided to majored in Finance and Mathematical Economics but this course was not helpful lack of Financial and Econ applications

Prof. Alar Toomre is one of the most brilliants minds at MIT. I took 18.03 Differential Equations and 18.04 Complex Variables. He focused on example from Physics. He really admired Srinivasa Ramanujan both are genius . He expand your horizons and how to understand Mathematics Sofía
a self-righteous, pretentious, arrogant prof who thinks hes too good to teach you

everything has been said: toomre is a math wiz and thinks everyone's like him. he neglects the basis and jumps to (very) complex applications. he looks nice and cheerful, but becomes quite hostile when contradicted. pretty unhelpful in general.
At first I thought I didn't know what was going on because I was too stupid.  Then I talked to someone who took a class with him 20 years ago and thought he was horrible and I knew it wasn't me.

review_highlights.comment: Thaler is a bit of a jerk. Sometimes he's nice, but it doesn't make up for the times he was insulting and disrespectful to students asking questions. He talked about attending Exeter Academy for high school, and his conduct indicates he has no understanding of students coming from different backgrounds.
Cool guy, bit of a memer. Does really difficult stuff but tends to explain things well. Was very nice to us during COVID.
8.033 was by far my most difficult class - took recitation with thaler, did well partly due to that.

review_highlights.comment: Professor Voldman does not do a good job actually teaching material. He relies on gimmicks and random useless demos but does not actually teach anything. The labs for the class are not helpful for learning material. I did not feel like I have learned anything from the class. Tests are ridiculously hard and basically impossible.
Smart guy. Expect a lot of work. Generally friendly. Sometimes tries to make things seem simpler than they really are.

Took an innovation course with him a few years ago. His manner and appearance are a little unortodox, but he's extremely interesting and fun, and useful if the course is of interest to you.

review_highlights.comment: Hands down the best professor I've had here. His enthusiasm for the subject material is infectious - Thermodynamics was one of the few classes I legitimately enjoyed, because he made me want to learn. He demands a lot, but if you accept the challenge, you'll learn a lot.
Hot only because of the ponytail...
Very challenging class, but an extremely knowledgeable professor
